description:
The **AWSSupport-CalculateEBSPerformanceMetrics** runbook helps diagnose Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) performance issues by calculating and publishing performance metrics to an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard. The dashboard displays the estimated average IOPS and throughput for a target Amazon EBS volume or all the volumes attached to the target Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) instance. For EC2 instances, it also shows the instance's average IOPS and throughput. For Nitro-based instances, the dashboard includes `ExceededCheck` metrics (`VolumeIOPSExceededCheck`, `VolumeThroughputExceededCheck`, `InstanceEBSIOPSExceededCheck`, `InstanceEBSThroughputExceededCheck`) that report whether an application attempted to exceed the EBS performance limits. The runbook outputs the link to the newly created Amazon CloudWatch dashboard that displays the relevant calculated Amazon CloudWatch metrics. The Amazon CloudWatch dashboard is created in your account with the name: `AWSSupport-<ResourceId>-EBS-Performance-<automation:EXECUTION_ID>`. The runbook requires the following permissions: `ec2:DescribeVolumes`, `ec2:DescribeInstances`, `ec2:DescribeInstanceTypes`, and `cloudwatch:PutDashboard`.

#### Important:
> * The creation of custom metrics and the CloudWatch dashboard may result in your account incurring additional costs. For more information see [Amazon CloudWatch pricing guide](https://aws.amazon.com/cloudwatch/pricing).

AutomationAssumeRole.description: (Optional) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) role that allows Systems Manager Automation to perform the actions on your behalf. If no role is specified, Systems Manager Automation uses the permissions of the user that starts this runbook.

ResourceId.description: (Required) The ID of the EC2 Instance or EBS Volume.

StartTime.description: (Required) The start time to view the data in CloudWatch. The time must be in the format `yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss` and in UTC.

EndTime.description: (Required) The end time to view the data in CloudWatch. The time must be in the format `yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss` and in UTC.

mainSteps.name: CheckResourceIdAndTimeStamps
mainSteps.description: Checks if the target resource specified in `ResourceId` exists, and the timestamps specified in `StartTime` and `EndTime` are valid.

mainSteps.name: CreateCloudWatchDashboard
mainSteps.description: Calculates the performance metrics and creates the Amazon CloudWatch dashboard.
